name: Kraftcor
description_short: Kraftcor Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution serving Hancock, Breckinridge, and Perry Counties with competitive rates and flexible lending options.
description_long:
Kraftcor Federal Credit Union was founded in 1972 by employees of Wescor and Western Kraft paper mills in Hawesville, Kentucky, with a federal charter approved on February 29, 1972. The credit union began with approximately $85,000 in assets and grew significantly under Curtis McCormick's management starting in 1975. Originally operating from a kitchen table and restaurant basement, Kraftcor moved to a dedicated facility in 1986 and constructed a new headquarters in June 2000. By the end of 2014, the credit union had grown to 1,920 members and $14.5 million in assets.

Kraftcor offers comprehensive financial services including savings accounts, checking accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), and a full range of loan products. They provide flexible financing options for members with good credit, bad credit, or no credit history. The credit union operates under a not-for-profit model, allowing them to offer competitive rates and tailored financial services. They also provide car buying services with pre-approval options and online banking access.

As a credit union, Kraftcor distinguishes itself through member ownership and the philosophy that "people are worth more than money." The institution expanded from being exclusively available to mill employees and their families to serving the broader community when it received community charter approval in 2002. Additional charter expansions in 2016 extended membership to all of Hancock and Breckinridge Counties in Kentucky and Perry County, Indiana. Members can join if they live, work, or worship in these counties or are immediate family of someone who does.

Kraftcor is a legitimate, federally-chartered credit union suitable for residents of its service area seeking member-owned banking with personalized service. However, geographic membership requirements restrict access to those in specific counties, and the website provides limited detail about specific product terms, rates, and fees. The credit union's emphasis on flexible lending is positive, though prospective members should contact them directly for comprehensive rate and product information. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Vehicle financing and car buying service with pre-approval
Financial products and services (details available upon contact)
pros: Federally chartered credit union established in 1972 with nearly 50 years of operational history
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure allows competitive rates and lower fees than traditional banks
Flexible lending approved for members with good credit, bad credit, or no credit history
Online banking and member application available for convenience
Car buying service with pre-approval options to help members secure vehicle financing
Expanded membership eligibility since 2002—now serving broader community beyond original mill employees
Located at permanent, dedicated facility in Hawesville, Kentucky with phone and in-person support available
cons: Geographic restrictions limit membership to Hancock, Breckinridge, and Perry Counties only
Website provides limited information about specific interest rates, fees, and loan terms
No mention of online account opening or mobile app availability despite online banking login option
Small institution with only 1,920 members (as of 2014) offers less branch network than larger banks
Limited details about deposit insurance coverage, insurance products, or account protections
best_for: Residents of Hancock, Breckinridge (Kentucky), or Perry (Indiana) Counties seeking member-owned banking
Borrowers with imperfect credit histories who need flexible lending approval
Community members looking for personalized financial service from a local, not-for-profit institution
Vehicle buyers wanting pre-approval and car buying assistance from a trusted local lender
diagnosis: Kraftcor is best for residents of its service area (Hancock, Breckinridge, and Perry Counties) who value member ownership, personalized service, and flexible lending standards. The main caveat is strict geographic membership requirements and limited online presence—prospective members must contact the credit union directly for current rates, specific product terms, and detailed underwriting criteria.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Member-owned credit union serving Hancock, Breckenridge, and Perry Counties with competitive rates.
